articles.headline: India, China Announce Agreement on Military Patrols along Line of Actual Control
articles.explainer:
• India and China have reached an understanding regarding military patrols along the disputed Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• The agreement aims to reduce friction and prevent inadvertent escalation in sensitive border areas.
• Details of the pact include guidelines for proximity of patrols and communication protocols.
• This development follows several rounds of military and diplomatic talks to de-escalate border tensions.
• The Ministry of External Affairs emphasized the importance of maintaining peace and tranquility for overall bilateral relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / Press Trust of India
prelimsPoints: The LAC is the de facto border between India and China.
The agreement focuses on military patrols to prevent face-offs.
Previous agreements like the 1993 and 1996 accords also addressed border management.
Key sectors of LAC include Ladakh, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the agreement in the context of India-China bilateral ties and regional stability.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such agreements given the history of border disputes.
Evaluate India's 'two-track' approach of diplomatic engagement and robust border infrastructure development.
Examine the role of international pressure and multilateral forums in influencing border dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Chinese soldiers patrolling near the Line of Actual Control in a mountainous region.
articles.trivia: The Line of Actual Control (LAC) is not demarcated on maps or on the ground, leading to differing perceptions of its alignment by both sides.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today largely upheld the constitutional validity of key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed petitions challenging certain aspects related to data fiduciary obligations and cross-border data transfers.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state/business interests.
• Justice D.Y. Chandrachud-led bench affirmed the necessity of a robust data protection framework in the digital age.
• The judgment provides clarity for businesses and government agencies in handling personal data.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
articles.source: Supreme Court of India / Legal News India
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act was enacted in August 2023.
It applies to digital personal data processed within India and some cases outside India.
The Act defines 'personal data', 'data fiduciary', and 'data principal'.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the digital economy and individual rights in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act, particularly regarding compliance for SMEs and data localization.
Compare India's data protection framework with global standards like GDPR.
Examine how the DPDP Act balances innovation, security, and privacy concerns.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, symbolizing a court ruling on data protection.
articles.trivia: India's journey towards a comprehensive data protection law began with the Srikrishna Committee Report in 2018.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New PLI Scheme to Boost Advanced Manufacturing and R&D
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme targeting advanced manufacturing sectors.
• The scheme aims to incentivize domestic and foreign investments in high-tech areas like semiconductors, advanced robotics, and green technologies.
• Financial outlays and eligibility criteria were detailed, focusing on enhancing India's global competitiveness and creating high-skilled jobs.
• This initiative is part of India's broader 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry highlighted the potential for significant economic growth and technological self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting

Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Investment models
articles.source: Ministry of Commerce and Industry / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: PLI schemes offer incentives based on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
First introduced in 2020, covering 14 key sectors.
This new scheme targets 'advanced manufacturing' and R&D.
Aims to reduce import dependence and boost exports.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of PLI schemes in boosting India's manufacturing sector and achieving economic self-reliance.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for advanced manufacturing.
Analyze how such schemes can address issues of unemployment and skill development.
Examine the potential impact on India's position in global supply chains, especially in critical technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Robotic arms on an assembly line, symbolizing advanced manufacturing.
articles.trivia: India aims to increase the manufacturing sector's contribution to GDP from around 17% to 25% by 2025.

articles.headline: India Hosts International Summit on Climate Resilience and Disaster Management
articles.explainer:
• India inaugurated a major international summit focused on enhancing climate resilience and disaster risk reduction.
• Delegates from over 50 countries, international organizations, and NGOs are participating.
• The summit aims to foster collaboration on early warning systems, sustainable infrastructure, and community-based resilience strategies.
• Prime Minister highlighted India's commitment to global climate action and its experiences in managing disasters.
• Key outcomes are expected to include a 'Delhi Declaration' outlining collective commitments for adaptation and resilience.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: India is highly vulnerable to various climate-related disasters.
The summit aligns with global goals like the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction.
Focus areas include resilient infrastructure and nature-based solutions.
India is a member of the Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure (CDRI).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and leadership in global climate action and disaster management efforts.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in building climate resilience and the need for international financial support.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current disaster management policies and strategies in India.
Suggest innovative approaches for integrating climate resilience into urban planning and rural development.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of people attending a conference, with a banner on climate resilience.
articles.trivia: India established the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) in 2005 under the Disaster Management Act.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating an autonomous return from space, a crucial step for future cost-effective space access.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's efforts to develop fully reusable orbital launch vehicles.
• The RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches and enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• Scientists lauded the precision and complexity involved in the autonomous navigation and landing sequence.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
articles.source: ISRO / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a series of experimental missions by ISRO.
The current test focused on autonomous landing capabilities.
Reusable rockets significantly reduce launch costs.
Other countries like USA (SpaceX) are also developing RLV technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how ISRO's RLV program can contribute to 'space for all' and foster international collaborations.
Examine the challenges in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
Evaluate the potential impact of advanced space technologies on India's national security and scientific research.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), was successfully conducted in 2016.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership Dialogue concluded with a joint statement emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security.
• Discussions focused on freedom of navigation, combating piracy, and ensuring regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• Both sides agreed to conduct joint naval exercises and intelligence sharing to counter emerging threats.
• India reiterated its commitment to ASEAN centrality and a rules-based international order.
• The dialogue also covered economic ties, connectivity projects, and cultural exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: ASEAN comprises 10 Southeast Asian nations.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Strategic Partner in 2012.
The dialogue underscores India's 'Act East Policy'.
Maritime security in the Indo-Pacific is a key area of cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in strengthening ties with ASEAN and its role in regional security architecture.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in addressing maritime security concerns in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine how growing geopolitical competition impacts regional groupings like ASEAN and India's strategic choices.
Evaluate the potential for greater economic integration between India and ASEAN member states.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from different countries conducting joint exercises in the ocean.
articles.trivia: The 'Act East Policy' is a successor to the 'Look East Policy' initiated in 1991.

articles.headline: National Commission for Women Launches National Initiative Against Cybercrime Targeting Women
articles.explainer:
• The National Commission for Women (NCW) launched a nationwide initiative to combat cybercrime against women and girls.
• The program includes awareness campaigns, capacity building for law enforcement, and a dedicated helpline for reporting online harassment.
• NCW chairperson highlighted the rising incidence of cyberstalking, online abuse, and digital exploitation.
• Collaborations with cyber security experts, tech companies, and state police forces are central to the initiative.
• The aim is to create a safer digital environment and empower women to report such crimes without fear.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism
articles.source: National Commission for Women / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: NCW is a statutory body established in 1992.
Its mandate is to review constitutional and legal safeguards for women.
The initiative addresses online safety for women.
Section 66E of IT Act deals with violation of privacy, often relevant to cybercrimes against women.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by women in the digital space and the need for a comprehensive approach to combat cybercrime.
Evaluate the role of statutory bodies like NCW in promoting gender justice and ensuring women's safety.
Analyze how technological advancements can be leveraged to prevent cybercrimes and assist victims.
Suggest policy reforms and societal changes required to create a safer and more inclusive digital environment for women.
articles.imageDescription: A woman using a laptop with a shield icon, symbolizing online safety.
articles.trivia: The Information Technology Act, 2000, is the primary law in India dealing with cybercrime and e-commerce.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework to Boost Agricultural Exports and Farmer Income Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare introduced a new policy framework aimed at significantly boosting India's agricultural exports.
• The policy focuses on value addition, infrastructure development for cold chains, and market access for Indian produce globally.
• It seeks to enhance farmer income by connecting them directly to international markets and promoting contract farming for exports.
• Emphasis is placed on promoting organic and GI-tagged agricultural products.
• The framework is expected to diversify India's export basket and strengthen its position as a global food supplier.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture / Business Standard
prelimsPoints: India is a major global producer of several agricultural commodities.
The policy aims to achieve sustainable and stable agricultural exports.
Focus on improving quality, packaging, and logistics.
APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) is involved in promoting exports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's agricultural sector to become a global export powerhouse and the challenges it faces.
Analyze how the new policy framework can address issues of farmer distress and enhance their income.
Evaluate the role of infrastructure development (cold chains, logistics) and value addition in boosting agricultural exports.
Examine the socio-economic implications of promoting contract farming and GI-tagged products for small and marginal farmers.
articles.imageDescription: Fresh fruits and vegetables being packed for export, with a map of the world in the background.
articles.trivia: India's agricultural exports crossed US$50 billion for the first time in FY 2021-22.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Tropical Disease
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a prevalent tropical disease.
• The vaccine candidate showed promising results in preclinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• This development holds immense potential for public health in India and other tropical regions globally.
• The research leveraged advanced biotechnological techniques and collaborative efforts between ICMR and private sector labs.
• Further clinical trials are planned to expedite the vaccine's availability to the public.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Bio-technology
articles.source: Indian Council of Medical Research / Nature India
prelimsPoints: Tropical diseases are prevalent in hot, humid climates.
ICMR (Indian Council of Medical Research) is the apex body for biomedical research in India.
Vaccine development involves several stages: research, preclinical, clinical (Phases 1, 2, 3), and approval.
Indigenous vaccine development enhances health security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of indigenous vaccine development for India's public health security and global health equity.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships and government funding in accelerating medical research and innovation.
Examine the challenges in vaccine distribution and accessibility, especially for populations in remote and vulnerable areas.
Evaluate India's potential to become a global leader in pharmaceutical and vaccine manufacturing, often referred to as the 'Pharmacy of the World'.
articles.imageDescription: Scientists in a laboratory examining vaccine samples under a microscope.
articles.trivia: India produces 60% of the world's vaccines and contributes 70% of WHO's essential immunogen supply.

articles.headline: Government Announces Ambitious New Targets for Green Hydrogen Production and Adoption
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy unveiled ambitious new targets for green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
• The revised targets aim to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.
• Incentives for research, manufacturing of electrolysers, and deployment in key industrial sectors were outlined.
• This move is critical for India to achieve its net-zero emissions goal and enhance energy security.
• Industry stakeholders welcomed the policy, emphasizing its potential to create new industries and green jobs.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / Livemint
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission in January 2023.
It aims to achieve 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
Hydrogen can be used as fuel, in fertilizers, refineries, and steel production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy transition, climate goals, and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption, including infrastructure, cost, and technology.
Evaluate the potential of India to become a global leader in green hydrogen, considering its renewable energy potential.
Examine the policy interventions required to create a robust ecosystem for green hydrogen, from R&D to market deployment.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image showing a factory emitting clean steam, with wind turbines and solar panels in the background, representing green hydrogen production.
articles.trivia: One kilogram of hydrogen contains roughly the same energy as 2.8 kilograms of gasoline.

articles.headline: Union Education Ministry Releases Report on Digital Learning Penetration in Rural India
articles.explainer:
• The Union Education Ministry released a comprehensive report detailing the penetration and impact of digital learning in rural areas across India.
• The report highlighted significant progress in expanding access to online educational resources, particularly post-pandemic.
• It also identified persistent challenges such as digital divide, lack of device access, and inconsistent internet connectivity.
• Recommendations included targeted government interventions, public-private partnerships, and localized content development.
• The findings will inform future policy decisions to ensure inclusive and equitable digital education.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Education / Press Information Bureau
prelimsPoints: The report assesses the reach of digital education initiatives like DIKSHA, SWAYAM.
Identifies gaps in infrastructure and affordability in rural areas.
Highlights the role of community learning centers and public Wi-Fi hotspots.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 emphasizes digital learning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the opportunities and challenges of leveraging digital learning to bridge educational disparities in rural India.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives in promoting digital literacy and access in remote areas.
Examine how public-private partnerships can contribute to creating a robust and equitable digital education ecosystem.
Suggest policy measures to address the digital divide and ensure that no student is left behind in the digital learning revolution.
articles.imageDescription: A group of rural children learning from a tablet in a village setting.
articles.trivia: BharatNet project aims to provide broadband connectivity to all Gram Panchayats in India.

articles.headline: Major Highway Corridor Project in Northeast India Achieves Significant Completion Milestone
articles.explainer:
• A vital highway corridor project connecting key regions in Northeast India has achieved a significant completion milestone, with over 70% of the work finalized.
• The project aims to enhance connectivity, reduce travel time, and boost economic activities in the region.
• It is part of the government's broader 'Act East Policy' and infrastructure thrust for the Northeast.
• The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways highlighted the engineering challenges overcome in mountainous terrain.
• Improved connectivity is expected to facilitate trade with neighboring countries and foster tourism.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.

Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
articles.source: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways / Assam Tribune
prelimsPoints: Northeast India is strategically important for its proximity to Southeast Asia.
Infrastructure development is crucial for economic integration and security.
The project is under initiatives like Bharatmala Pariyojana or SARDP-NE (Special Accelerated Road Development Programme for North Eastern Region).
It involves construction through challenging geographical terrains.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of infrastructure development in Northeast India for national security, economic integration, and regional connectivity.
Analyze the geographical and socio-economic challenges in implementing large-scale infrastructure projects in the region.
Evaluate how improved connectivity can boost tourism, trade, and employment opportunities for the local population.
Examine the role of 'Act East Policy' in driving infrastructure development and fostering closer ties with ASEAN nations.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed highway winding through lush green mountains in Northeast India.
articles.trivia: The Northeast Region accounts for about 8% of India's geographical area and 3.7% of its population.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers New Historical Artifacts at Prominent Ancient Site
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of several significant historical artifacts during ongoing excavations at a prominent ancient site.
• The findings include pottery, tools, and structural remains that shed new light on an ancient civilization's daily life and culture.
• Experts believe the artifacts belong to a previously less-understood period, offering valuable insights into India's rich historical tapestry.
• The discovery is expected to necessitate a re-evaluation of certain historical timelines and cultural influences.
• Conservation efforts are underway to preserve the newly unearthed treasures for future study and public display.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors /contributions from different parts of the country.
articles.source: Archaeological Survey of India / Deccan Herald
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Artifacts like pottery, tools, and coins provide clues about past societies.
The discovery could relate to various periods like Harappan, Vedic, Mauryan, Gupta, etc.
Carbon dating is often used to determine the age of artifacts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding its cultural evolution.
Analyze the role of the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Examine the challenges faced in archaeological excavations and the ethical considerations involved in handling historical artifacts.
Evaluate how such discoveries contribute to national identity and promote cultural tourism.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery fragments.
articles.trivia: ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who became its first Director-General.

articles.headline: Indian Navy Conducts Major Maritime Exercise in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy conducted a large-scale maritime exercise in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR), involving a significant number of warships, submarines, and aircraft.
• The exercise focused on enhancing operational readiness, interoperability, and maritime domain awareness among various units.
• Scenarios included anti-submarine warfare, air defense, and maritime interdiction operations.
• The Chief of Naval Staff emphasized India's commitment to securing its maritime interests and contributing to regional stability.
• The exercise also demonstrated India's growing capabilities as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
articles.source: Ministry of Defence / Hindustan Times
prelimsPoints: The Indian Ocean is crucial for India's trade and energy security.
Indian Navy conducts regular exercises to maintain operational readiness.
It collaborates with navies of friendly nations (e.g., QUAD, IONS).
India's maritime strategy includes safeguarding its EEZ and sea lanes of communication.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving geopolitical significance of the Indian Ocean Region and India's role as a net security provider.
Analyze the challenges and threats to maritime security in the IOR, including piracy, terrorism, and growing naval presence of extra-regional powers.
Evaluate the modernization efforts of the Indian Navy and their implications for India's strategic autonomy.
Examine how India's maritime diplomacy and bilateral/multilateral naval exercises contribute to regional stability and cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Navy warship sailing during a maritime exercise, with other vessels in the background.
articles.trivia: The Indian Navy's largest exercise is 'TROPEX' (Theatre Level Operational Readiness Exercise).

articles.headline: SEBI Introduces Stricter Regulations for High-Frequency Trading to Ensure Market Fairness
articles.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) announced new, stricter regulations for High-Frequency Trading (HFT) in Indian stock markets.
• The measures aim to address concerns about market manipulation, unfair advantage, and system vulnerabilities associated with HFT.
• New rules include enhanced surveillance, circuit breaker mechanisms, and stricter order-to-trade ratios.
• SEBI emphasized the need to balance technological innovation with market integrity and investor protection.
• The move is expected to create a more level playing field for all market participants, including retail investors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting

Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Investment models
articles.source: SEBI / Business Today
prelimsPoints: SEBI is the regulatory body for the securities market in India.
HFT involves using complex algorithms and high-speed infrastructure to execute trades.
Concerns exist regarding 'flash crashes' and front-running.
New regulations aim to increase transparency and fairness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the benefits and risks associated with High-Frequency Trading (HFT) in modern financial markets.
Analyze the rationale behind SEBI's stricter regulations for HFT and their potential impact on market efficiency and liquidity.
Evaluate the challenges faced by regulators in balancing technological innovation with market integrity and investor protection.
Examine how algorithmic trading can contribute to or detract from the stability of India's capital markets.
articles.imageDescription: A stock market ticker displaying fluctuating numbers, with graphs representing high-frequency trading.
articles.trivia: HFT firms can execute thousands of trades in a fraction of a second.

articles.headline: Editorial: India's Enduring Legacy in Shaping the G20 Agenda
articles.explainer:
• This editorial reflects on India's significant contributions and leadership in shaping the G20 agenda, even after its presidency.
• It highlights India's success in bringing global attention to issues like sustainable development, digital public infrastructure, and climate finance for developing nations.
• The editorial praises India's diplomatic prowess in achieving consensus on complex global challenges.
• It argues that India's 'Voice of the Global South' narrative has gained significant traction, influencing future G20 priorities.
• The piece emphasizes the continued importance of India's engagement for multilateral cooperation and global governance.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
articles.source: The Hindu (Editorial)
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the impact of India's G20 Presidency on global governance and its implications for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss how India's focus on issues like digital public infrastructure and climate finance can bridge the North-South divide within global forums.
Analyze the concept of 'Voice of the Global South' and its growing relevance in shaping international discourse.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in sustaining its influence within the G20 and other multilateral institutions.
articles.imageDescription: National flags of G20 member countries arranged in a circle, with India's flag prominently featured.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

articles.headline: Study Highlights Increasing Urban Heat Island Effect in Indian Cities, Calls for Mitigation
articles.explainer:
• A new study published by an Indian research institute highlights the increasing 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect in major Indian cities.
• The study attributes the phenomenon to rapid urbanization, loss of green cover, and extensive use of heat-absorbing materials in construction.
• It warns of severe health implications, increased energy consumption for cooling, and reduced outdoor comfort for urban dwellers.
• Researchers recommend immediate mitigation strategies, including green infrastructure, cool pavements, and climate-responsive urban planning.
• The findings underscore the urgency for sustainable urban development policies to combat rising temperatures.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
articles.source: Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: UHI effect refers to urban areas being significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas.
Causes include heat absorption by concrete, asphalt, and lack of vegetation.
Consequences include higher energy demand, air pollution, and health risks.
Mitigation involves increasing green spaces, using reflective materials, and water bodies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island effect in Indian cities, particularly in the context of rapid urbanization.
Analyze the effectiveness of various mitigation strategies, such as green infrastructure and cool roofs, in combating UHI.
Examine the public health and economic implications of rising urban temperatures.
Suggest integrated urban planning approaches that can foster climate resilience and improve liveability in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a dense urban area contrasted with surrounding green fields, showing temperature variations.
articles.trivia: The UHI effect can make cities several degrees Celsius warmer than their rural surroundings, especially at night.

articles.headline: RBI Report Highlights Growth in Digital Banking Services, Calls for Deeper Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released a report indicating significant growth in digital banking services and transactions across the country.
• The report lauded the expansion of UPI, mobile banking, and internet banking, contributing to greater financial access.
• However, it also underscored the need for deeper financial inclusion, particularly among marginalized communities and in remote areas.
• Recommendations included enhancing digital literacy, improving last-mile connectivity, and designing user-friendly interfaces.
• The RBI emphasized its commitment to promoting innovation while ensuring security and consumer protection in the digital financial ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
articles.source: Reserve Bank of India / Financial Express
prelimsPoints: RBI is India's central bank and financial regulator.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
Financial inclusion aims to provide access to financial services to all sections of society.
PMJDY (Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana) is a key government initiative for financial inclusion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative role of digital banking in achieving financial inclusion and economic empowerment in India.
Analyze the challenges in extending digital financial services to the unbanked and underbanked populations, especially in rural and remote areas.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and RBI policies in promoting digital literacy and secure digital transactions.
Examine the potential risks associated with rapid digitization of finance, such as cyber security threats and digital divide.
articles.imageDescription: A hand holding a smartphone with a banking app open, showing digital transactions.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: Global FDI Trends Report: India Remains Attractive Despite Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• A global report on Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) trends indicated that India remains a highly attractive destination for investors despite prevailing global economic headwinds.
• The report highlighted India's robust economic growth, large domestic market, and ongoing structural reforms as key drivers.
• While global FDI flows saw a modest decline, India continued to attract significant investments in manufacturing, digital services, and infrastructure.
• Challenges cited included geopolitical uncertainties and supply chain disruptions, which India is actively addressing.
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) expressed optimism about continued FDI inflows.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
articles.source: UNCTAD / DPIIT / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: FDI is a crucial source of non-debt financial resources for India's economic development.
India has consistently ranked among the top FDI destinations globally.
Key sectors attracting FDI include services, computer software and hardware, trading, and infrastructure.
DPIIT is responsible for the formulation and implementation of FDI policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's continued attractiveness as an FDI destination amid global economic uncertainties.
Discuss the role of government policies and structural reforms in facilitating FDI inflows and promoting economic growth.
Evaluate the impact of FDI on employment generation, technology transfer, and overall industrial development in India.
Examine the challenges India faces in sustaining high FDI inflows and integrating into global value chains.
articles.imageDescription: A chart showing rising FDI trends for India, with a world map background.
articles.trivia: Mauritius, Singapore, USA, Netherlands, and Japan are among the top five sources of FDI in India.

articles.headline: India Rejects Global Hunger Index Ranking, Citing Methodological Flaws
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Women and Child Development rejected its ranking in the recently released Global Hunger Index (GHI) report.
• The government cited methodological flaws and questioned the reliance on a small sample size for critical indicators like child wasting.
• India emphasized its robust efforts through various welfare schemes to combat malnutrition and food insecurity.
• The GHI report had placed India in a lower position, prompting a strong official rebuttal.
• This annual debate highlights ongoing discussions about appropriate metrics for measuring hunger and malnutrition.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development / PIB
prelimsPoints: GHI is published annually by Concern Worldwide and Welthungerhilfe.
It uses four indicators: undernourishment, child wasting, child stunting, and child mortality.
India often questions the methodology, especially regarding child wasting data sources.
Government schemes like Mid-Day Meal, ICDS, and Poshan Abhiyaan address malnutrition.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the methodology of the Global Hunger Index and India's concerns regarding its ranking.
Discuss the multi-dimensional nature of hunger and malnutrition in India and the effectiveness of government interventions to address them.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to child wasting and stunting in India.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to improve India's nutritional outcomes, integrating health, sanitation, and food security measures.
articles.imageDescription: A child being measured for height and weight, symbolizing efforts to combat malnutrition.
articles.trivia: India is home to a significant portion of the world's malnourished children.

highlights: India and China announced an agreement on military patrols along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) to de-escalate border tensions.
Supreme Court largely upheld key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, dismissing challenges to its constitutional validity.
Union Government unveiled a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aimed at boosting advanced manufacturing and R&D in high-tech sectors.
ISRO successfully tested its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator, marking a milestone in cost-effective space access.
India hosted an international summit on climate resilience and disaster management, fostering global collaboration on adaptation strategies.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Line of Actual Control (LAC) between India and China, consider the following statements:
1. The LAC is a clearly demarcated boundary recognized by both India and China.
2. Recent agreements aim to establish communication protocols for military patrols along the LAC.
3. The LAC primarily runs through the states of Jammu & Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The LAC is the de facto boundary and is not clearly demarcated or mutually agreed upon by both sides, leading to differing perceptions. Statement 2 is correct, as the recent agreement focuses on communication and protocols for military patrols to reduce friction. Statement 3 is incorrect. The LAC primarily runs through Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Sikkim, and Arunachal Pradesh. Jammu & Kashmir is now a Union Territory, and the LAC includes parts of Ladakh.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for the regulation of the securities market in India?
options: (c) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
prelimsMocks.explanation: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) is the statutory regulatory body for the securities market in India. The RBI regulates banks and monetary policy. The Ministry of Finance is a government ministry, and NSE is a stock exchange.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Green Hydrogen:
1. It is produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
2. India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to achieve 5 Million Metric Tonnes of production capacity by 2030.
3. It can be used in sectors like fertilizers, refineries, and steel production.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. Green hydrogen is indeed produced via electrolysis powered by renewables. India's mission has a target of 5 MMT by 2030, and it has diverse industrial applications for decarbonization.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect in cities is primarily caused by:
options: (b) High levels of air pollution from vehicle emissions
(c) Rapid urbanization, loss of green cover, and heat-absorbing construction materials
(d) Increased solar radiation due to depletion of the ozone layer
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Rapid urbanization, loss of green cover, and heat-absorbing construction materials
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Urban Heat Island effect refers to urban areas being significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas. This is mainly due to dense construction (concrete, asphalt) that absorbs and re-emits heat, lack of vegetation for evaporative cooling, and heat generated by human activities. While air pollution contributes, it's not the primary cause of the UHI effect itself.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT an indicator used in the calculation of the Global Hunger Index (GHI)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Global Hunger Index (GHI) uses four indicators: Undernourishment, Child Wasting (proportion of children under five who have low weight for their height), Child Stunting (proportion of children under five who have low height for their age), and Child Mortality (mortality rate of children under five). Maternal Mortality Rate is not a direct indicator for GHI.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic significance of the recent India-China agreement on military patrols along the Line of Actual Control (LAC). What are the inherent challenges in maintaining peace and tranquility along the disputed border, and how can these be addressed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests; GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the agreement and its immediate goal. Discuss strategic significance (de-escalation, CBMs, bilateral ties). Detail inherent challenges (differing perceptions, infrastructure build-up, trust deficit, past incidents). Suggest addressing mechanisms (robust communication, clear demarcation, political will, multilateral engagement, economic interdependence).

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is a landmark legislation for India. Analyze how the Supreme Court's recent upholding of its key provisions contributes to balancing individual privacy rights with national interests and technological innovation. What are the implementation challenges, and how can they be overcome?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; GS3: Awareness in the fields of IT, Computers.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly explain the DPDP Act and the SC ruling's significance. Discuss the balance between privacy, state/business interests, and innovation. Detail implementation challenges (compliance for SMEs, cross-border data flows, data protection board capacity, digital literacy). Suggest solutions (awareness campaigns, regulatory clarity, infra development, skill building).

mainsMocks.question: India's ambition to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export is a key component of its energy transition strategy. Evaluate the opportunities and challenges in achieving this goal. What policy measures are essential to foster a robust green hydrogen ecosystem in the country?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy; Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Define green hydrogen and India's ambition. Discuss opportunities (abundant renewable energy, energy security, decarbonization, economic growth, job creation). Detail challenges (high production cost, technology scale-up, infrastructure, water availability, market demand). Suggest policy measures (PLI schemes, R&D funding, demand creation, regulatory framework, international collaboration).

quickBytes.text: The Line of Actual Control (LAC) is a de facto border and not a mutually agreed, demarcated line between India and China.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes offer incentives based on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
The Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction (2015-2030) guides global efforts in disaster management.
ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) comprises 10 member states in Southeast Asia.
The National Commission for Women (NCW) is a statutory body established in 1992.
Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using electricity generated from renewable sources.
The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect describes cities being warmer than surrounding rural areas.
Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.

GS1.headline: National Commission for Women Launches National Initiative Against Cybercrime Targeting Women
GS1.explainer:
• The National Commission for Women (NCW) launched a nationwide initiative to combat cybercrime against women and girls.
• The program includes awareness campaigns, capacity building for law enforcement, and a dedicated helpline for reporting online harassment.
• NCW chairperson highlighted the rising incidence of cyberstalking, online abuse, and digital exploitation.
• Collaborations with cyber security experts, tech companies, and state police forces are central to the initiative.
• The aim is to create a safer digital environment and empower women to report such crimes without fear.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism
GS1.source: National Commission for Women / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: NCW is a statutory body established in 1992.
Its mandate is to review constitutional and legal safeguards for women.
The initiative addresses online safety for women.
Section 66E of IT Act deals with violation of privacy, often relevant to cybercrimes against women.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by women in the digital space and the need for a comprehensive approach to combat cybercrime.
Evaluate the role of statutory bodies like NCW in promoting gender justice and ensuring women's safety.
Analyze how technological advancements can be leveraged to prevent cybercrimes and assist victims.
Suggest policy reforms and societal changes required to create a safer and more inclusive digital environment for women.
GS1.imageDescription: A woman using a laptop with a shield icon, symbolizing online safety.
GS1.trivia: The Information Technology Act, 2000, is the primary law in India dealing with cybercrime and e-commerce.

GS1.headline: Major Highway Corridor Project in Northeast India Achieves Significant Completion Milestone
GS1.explainer:
• A vital highway corridor project connecting key regions in Northeast India has achieved a significant completion milestone, with over 70% of the work finalized.
• The project aims to enhance connectivity, reduce travel time, and boost economic activities in the region.
• It is part of the government's broader 'Act East Policy' and infrastructure thrust for the Northeast.
• The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways highlighted the engineering challenges overcome in mountainous terrain.
• Improved connectivity is expected to facilitate trade with neighboring countries and foster tourism.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.

Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
GS1.source: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways / Assam Tribune
prelimsPoints: Northeast India is strategically important for its proximity to Southeast Asia.
Infrastructure development is crucial for economic integration and security.
The project is under initiatives like Bharatmala Pariyojana or SARDP-NE (Special Accelerated Road Development Programme for North Eastern Region).
It involves construction through challenging geographical terrains.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of infrastructure development in Northeast India for national security, economic integration, and regional connectivity.
Analyze the geographical and socio-economic challenges in implementing large-scale infrastructure projects in the region.
Evaluate how improved connectivity can boost tourism, trade, and employment opportunities for the local population.
Examine the role of 'Act East Policy' in driving infrastructure development and fostering closer ties with ASEAN nations.
GS1.imageDescription: A newly constructed highway winding through lush green mountains in Northeast India.
GS1.trivia: The Northeast Region accounts for about 8% of India's geographical area and 3.7% of its population.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers New Historical Artifacts at Prominent Ancient Site
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of several significant historical artifacts during ongoing excavations at a prominent ancient site.
• The findings include pottery, tools, and structural remains that shed new light on an ancient civilization's daily life and culture.
• Experts believe the artifacts belong to a previously less-understood period, offering valuable insights into India's rich historical tapestry.
• The discovery is expected to necessitate a re-evaluation of certain historical timelines and cultural influences.
• Conservation efforts are underway to preserve the newly unearthed treasures for future study and public display.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors /contributions from different parts of the country.
GS1.source: Archaeological Survey of India / Deccan Herald
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Artifacts like pottery, tools, and coins provide clues about past societies.
The discovery could relate to various periods like Harappan, Vedic, Mauryan, Gupta, etc.
Carbon dating is often used to determine the age of artifacts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding its cultural evolution.
Analyze the role of the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Examine the challenges faced in archaeological excavations and the ethical considerations involved in handling historical artifacts.
Evaluate how such discoveries contribute to national identity and promote cultural tourism.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery fragments.
GS1.trivia: ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who became its first Director-General.

GS1.headline: Study Highlights Increasing Urban Heat Island Effect in Indian Cities, Calls for Mitigation
GS1.explainer:
• A new study published by an Indian research institute highlights the increasing 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect in major Indian cities.
• The study attributes the phenomenon to rapid urbanization, loss of green cover, and extensive use of heat-absorbing materials in construction.
• It warns of severe health implications, increased energy consumption for cooling, and reduced outdoor comfort for urban dwellers.
• Researchers recommend immediate mitigation strategies, including green infrastructure, cool pavements, and climate-responsive urban planning.
• The findings underscore the urgency for sustainable urban development policies to combat rising temperatures.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
GS1.source: Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: UHI effect refers to urban areas being significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas.
Causes include heat absorption by concrete, asphalt, and lack of vegetation.
Consequences include higher energy demand, air pollution, and health risks.
Mitigation involves increasing green spaces, using reflective materials, and water bodies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island effect in Indian cities, particularly in the context of rapid urbanization.
Analyze the effectiveness of various mitigation strategies, such as green infrastructure and cool roofs, in combating UHI.
Examine the public health and economic implications of rising urban temperatures.
Suggest integrated urban planning approaches that can foster climate resilience and improve liveability in Indian cities.
GS1.imageDescription: An aerial view of a dense urban area contrasted with surrounding green fields, showing temperature variations.
GS1.trivia: The UHI effect can make cities several degrees Celsius warmer than their rural surroundings, especially at night.

GS2.headline: India, China Announce Agreement on Military Patrols along Line of Actual Control
GS2.explainer:
• India and China have reached an understanding regarding military patrols along the disputed Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• The agreement aims to reduce friction and prevent inadvertent escalation in sensitive border areas.
• Details of the pact include guidelines for proximity of patrols and communication protocols.
• This development follows several rounds of military and diplomatic talks to de-escalate border tensions.
• The Ministry of External Affairs emphasized the importance of maintaining peace and tranquility for overall bilateral relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / Press Trust of India
prelimsPoints: The LAC is the de facto border between India and China.
The agreement focuses on military patrols to prevent face-offs.
Previous agreements like the 1993 and 1996 accords also addressed border management.
Key sectors of LAC include Ladakh, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the agreement in the context of India-China bilateral ties and regional stability.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such agreements given the history of border disputes.
Evaluate India's 'two-track' approach of diplomatic engagement and robust border infrastructure development.
Examine the role of international pressure and multilateral forums in influencing border dynamics.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Chinese soldiers patrolling near the Line of Actual Control in a mountainous region.
GS2.trivia: The Line of Actual Control (LAC) is not demarcated on maps or on the ground, leading to differing perceptions of its alignment by both sides.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today largely upheld the constitutional validity of key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed petitions challenging certain aspects related to data fiduciary obligations and cross-border data transfers.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state/business interests.
• Justice D.Y. Chandrachud-led bench affirmed the necessity of a robust data protection framework in the digital age.
• The judgment provides clarity for businesses and government agencies in handling personal data.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
GS2.source: Supreme Court of India / Legal News India
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act was enacted in August 2023.
It applies to digital personal data processed within India and some cases outside India.
The Act defines 'personal data', 'data fiduciary', and 'data principal'.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the digital economy and individual rights in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act, particularly regarding compliance for SMEs and data localization.
Compare India's data protection framework with global standards like GDPR.
Examine how the DPDP Act balances innovation, security, and privacy concerns.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, symbolizing a court ruling on data protection.
GS2.trivia: India's journey towards a comprehensive data protection law began with the Srikrishna Committee Report in 2018.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership Dialogue concluded with a joint statement emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security.
• Discussions focused on freedom of navigation, combating piracy, and ensuring regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• Both sides agreed to conduct joint naval exercises and intelligence sharing to counter emerging threats.
• India reiterated its commitment to ASEAN centrality and a rules-based international order.
• The dialogue also covered economic ties, connectivity projects, and cultural exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: ASEAN comprises 10 Southeast Asian nations.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Strategic Partner in 2012.
The dialogue underscores India's 'Act East Policy'.
Maritime security in the Indo-Pacific is a key area of cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in strengthening ties with ASEAN and its role in regional security architecture.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in addressing maritime security concerns in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine how growing geopolitical competition impacts regional groupings like ASEAN and India's strategic choices.
Evaluate the potential for greater economic integration between India and ASEAN member states.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from different countries conducting joint exercises in the ocean.
GS2.trivia: The 'Act East Policy' is a successor to the 'Look East Policy' initiated in 1991.

GS2.headline: Union Education Ministry Releases Report on Digital Learning Penetration in Rural India
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Education Ministry released a comprehensive report detailing the penetration and impact of digital learning in rural areas across India.
• The report highlighted significant progress in expanding access to online educational resources, particularly post-pandemic.
• It also identified persistent challenges such as digital divide, lack of device access, and inconsistent internet connectivity.
• Recommendations included targeted government interventions, public-private partnerships, and localized content development.
• The findings will inform future policy decisions to ensure inclusive and equitable digital education.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Education / Press Information Bureau
prelimsPoints: The report assesses the reach of digital education initiatives like DIKSHA, SWAYAM.
Identifies gaps in infrastructure and affordability in rural areas.
Highlights the role of community learning centers and public Wi-Fi hotspots.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 emphasizes digital learning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the opportunities and challenges of leveraging digital learning to bridge educational disparities in rural India.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives in promoting digital literacy and access in remote areas.
Examine how public-private partnerships can contribute to creating a robust and equitable digital education ecosystem.
Suggest policy measures to address the digital divide and ensure that no student is left behind in the digital learning revolution.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of rural children learning from a tablet in a village setting.
GS2.trivia: BharatNet project aims to provide broadband connectivity to all Gram Panchayats in India.

GS2.headline: Editorial: India's Enduring Legacy in Shaping the G20 Agenda
GS2.explainer:
• This editorial reflects on India's significant contributions and leadership in shaping the G20 agenda, even after its presidency.
• It highlights India's success in bringing global attention to issues like sustainable development, digital public infrastructure, and climate finance for developing nations.
• The editorial praises India's diplomatic prowess in achieving consensus on complex global challenges.
• It argues that India's 'Voice of the Global South' narrative has gained significant traction, influencing future G20 priorities.
• The piece emphasizes the continued importance of India's engagement for multilateral cooperation and global governance.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the impact of India's G20 Presidency on global governance and its implications for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss how India's focus on issues like digital public infrastructure and climate finance can bridge the North-South divide within global forums.
Analyze the concept of 'Voice of the Global South' and its growing relevance in shaping international discourse.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in sustaining its influence within the G20 and other multilateral institutions.
GS2.imageDescription: National flags of G20 member countries arranged in a circle, with India's flag prominently featured.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

GS2.headline: India Rejects Global Hunger Index Ranking, Citing Methodological Flaws
GS2.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Women and Child Development rejected its ranking in the recently released Global Hunger Index (GHI) report.
• The government cited methodological flaws and questioned the reliance on a small sample size for critical indicators like child wasting.
• India emphasized its robust efforts through various welfare schemes to combat malnutrition and food insecurity.
• The GHI report had placed India in a lower position, prompting a strong official rebuttal.
• This annual debate highlights ongoing discussions about appropriate metrics for measuring hunger and malnutrition.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development / PIB
prelimsPoints: GHI is published annually by Concern Worldwide and Welthungerhilfe.
It uses four indicators: undernourishment, child wasting, child stunting, and child mortality.
India often questions the methodology, especially regarding child wasting data sources.
Government schemes like Mid-Day Meal, ICDS, and Poshan Abhiyaan address malnutrition.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the methodology of the Global Hunger Index and India's concerns regarding its ranking.
Discuss the multi-dimensional nature of hunger and malnutrition in India and the effectiveness of government interventions to address them.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to child wasting and stunting in India.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to improve India's nutritional outcomes, integrating health, sanitation, and food security measures.
GS2.imageDescription: A child being measured for height and weight, symbolizing efforts to combat malnutrition.
GS2.trivia: India is home to a significant portion of the world's malnourished children.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New PLI Scheme to Boost Advanced Manufacturing and R&D
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme targeting advanced manufacturing sectors.
• The scheme aims to incentivize domestic and foreign investments in high-tech areas like semiconductors, advanced robotics, and green technologies.
• Financial outlays and eligibility criteria were detailed, focusing on enhancing India's global competitiveness and creating high-skilled jobs.
• This initiative is part of India's broader 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry highlighted the potential for significant economic growth and technological self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting

Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Investment models
GS3.source: Ministry of Commerce and Industry / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: PLI schemes offer incentives based on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
First introduced in 2020, covering 14 key sectors.
This new scheme targets 'advanced manufacturing' and R&D.
Aims to reduce import dependence and boost exports.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of PLI schemes in boosting India's manufacturing sector and achieving economic self-reliance.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for advanced manufacturing.
Analyze how such schemes can address issues of unemployment and skill development.
Examine the potential impact on India's position in global supply chains, especially in critical technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Robotic arms on an assembly line, symbolizing advanced manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: India aims to increase the manufacturing sector's contribution to GDP from around 17% to 25% by 2025.

GS3.headline: India Hosts International Summit on Climate Resilience and Disaster Management
GS3.explainer:
• India inaugurated a major international summit focused on enhancing climate resilience and disaster risk reduction.
• Delegates from over 50 countries, international organizations, and NGOs are participating.
• The summit aims to foster collaboration on early warning systems, sustainable infrastructure, and community-based resilience strategies.
• Prime Minister highlighted India's commitment to global climate action and its experiences in managing disasters.
• Key outcomes are expected to include a 'Delhi Declaration' outlining collective commitments for adaptation and resilience.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: India is highly vulnerable to various climate-related disasters.
The summit aligns with global goals like the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction.
Focus areas include resilient infrastructure and nature-based solutions.
India is a member of the Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure (CDRI).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and leadership in global climate action and disaster management efforts.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in building climate resilience and the need for international financial support.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current disaster management policies and strategies in India.
Suggest innovative approaches for integrating climate resilience into urban planning and rural development.
GS3.imageDescription: A diverse group of people attending a conference, with a banner on climate resilience.
GS3.trivia: India established the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) in 2005 under the Disaster Management Act.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating an autonomous return from space, a crucial step for future cost-effective space access.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's efforts to develop fully reusable orbital launch vehicles.
• The RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches and enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• Scientists lauded the precision and complexity involved in the autonomous navigation and landing sequence.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a series of experimental missions by ISRO.
The current test focused on autonomous landing capabilities.
Reusable rockets significantly reduce launch costs.
Other countries like USA (SpaceX) are also developing RLV technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how ISRO's RLV program can contribute to 'space for all' and foster international collaborations.
Examine the challenges in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
Evaluate the potential impact of advanced space technologies on India's national security and scientific research.
GS3.imageDescription: An artist's rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle descending for a landing.
GS3.trivia: The first RLV-TD mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), was successfully conducted in 2016.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework to Boost Agricultural Exports and Farmer Income Unveiled
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare introduced a new policy framework aimed at significantly boosting India's agricultural exports.
• The policy focuses on value addition, infrastructure development for cold chains, and market access for Indian produce globally.
• It seeks to enhance farmer income by connecting them directly to international markets and promoting contract farming for exports.
• Emphasis is placed on promoting organic and GI-tagged agricultural products.
• The framework is expected to diversify India's export basket and strengthen its position as a global food supplier.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: India is a major global producer of several agricultural commodities.
The policy aims to achieve sustainable and stable agricultural exports.
Focus on improving quality, packaging, and logistics.
APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) is involved in promoting exports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's agricultural sector to become a global export powerhouse and the challenges it faces.
Analyze how the new policy framework can address issues of farmer distress and enhance their income.
Evaluate the role of infrastructure development (cold chains, logistics) and value addition in boosting agricultural exports.
Examine the socio-economic implications of promoting contract farming and GI-tagged products for small and marginal farmers.
GS3.imageDescription: Fresh fruits and vegetables being packed for export, with a map of the world in the background.
GS3.trivia: India's agricultural exports crossed US$50 billion for the first time in FY 2021-22.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Tropical Disease
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a prevalent tropical disease.
• The vaccine candidate showed promising results in preclinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• This development holds immense potential for public health in India and other tropical regions globally.
• The research leveraged advanced biotechnological techniques and collaborative efforts between ICMR and private sector labs.
• Further clinical trials are planned to expedite the vaccine's availability to the public.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
